title: ficus aegeirophylla
intro: Ficus aegeirophylla, also known as Malay tea tree, is a species of fig plant native to Malaysia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus aegeirophylla include reducing inflammation, fighting against bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting skin health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into a tea or infused in hot water to release its medicinal properties, and it is also used in some traditional remedies as a poultice to treat skin conditions.
